# c43 pool/GMP leak-scan baseline
# Known findings on upstream master, generated by scripts/test/run-leakscan.sh.
# The gate (scripts/test/run-leakscan.sh) fails on any finding NOT listed here.
# Format: <mode> <kind> <identifier>
#
# Re-verified unchanged against upstream master 1624e09b3 (gate PASSED, no
# regeneration needed). Generated against 87c70c77a (UPDATE_BASELINE=1) with two
# changes from the a361b6797 baseline, each root-caused rather than re-blessed:
#
# 1. REMOVED (fixed upstream): CRASH item=1767 2368 2369 2370 2371 2372 2373
#    2377 2378 2384 2735 - the equation solver/grapher cluster (fnEqSolvGraph
#    realSlv/cpxSlv/Draw, fnEqCalc, fn1stDerivEq, fn2ndDerivEq, fnComplexPlot).
#    Upstream 3bd0f13ea "fix crash running cpxSlv/Draw/Calc-f with no equation
#    defined" guards the NULL allFormulae dereference in parseEquation and
#    fnEqSolvGraph. Verified by pinning the lane (UPSTREAM_COMMIT): at 3bd0f13ea^
#    all 11 still crash; at 3bd0f13ea exactly these 11 stop, and the same 11 are
#    absent at 87c70c77a. Removed because the finding is fixed, not because the
#    baseline was noisy.
#
# 2. ADDED: CRASH item=994 (fnSolveVar "SOLVE Var") and item=1002 (fnIntVar
#    "fdx VarL") - SIGSEGV on all four stack types. Same documented class as the
#    other CRASH entries: a handler called out of keyboard/menu context. Both do
#      getNthString(dynamicSoftmenu[softmenuStack[0].softmenuId].menuContent,
#                   dynamicMenuItem)
#    (solve.c fnSolveVar, integrate.c fnIntVar) without guarding dynamicMenuItem.
#    c47.c documents -1 as "no dynamic menu item is selected", and every other
#    consumer honours it (programmableMenu.c ==-1; lblGtoXeq.c and print.c >=0);
#    these two index with it instead. Bisected (git bisect run over the lane) to
#    upstream 82d580db8 "default dynamicMenuItem to -1 so the first XEQ does not
#    divert": that commit did not create the flaw, it made the sentinel take its
#    documented value - the previous default 0 was a valid index that masked the
#    missing guard. Not user-reachable: both items are CAT_NONE (in no catalog,
#    so not XEQ/ASN reachable) and are only invoked from the MVAR dynamic
#    softmenu, where the key press sets dynamicMenuItem >= 0. The scanner calls
#    them headless with no menu, so it sees -1. Baselined as an out-of-context
#    artifact; a guard in those two functions would retire these entries.
#
# Triage (see __DEV/reports/REPORT-4-BUGS.md):
# - leakscan LEAK item=1202/1203/1465/1889 - persistent state
#   (named vars / equations / refresh), not orphan leaks.
# - CRASH item=... - handlers called out of keyboard/menu context.
# - keyscan CRASH seq=integrate_pgm_x20 - REAL SIGSEGV, tracked pending a
#   c43 fix branch; not a scanner out-of-context artifact.
# Remove a line here only when the underlying finding is fixed upstream.

keyscan CRASH seq=integrate_pgm_x20
leakscan CRASH item=1002
leakscan CRASH item=1197
leakscan CRASH item=1483
leakscan CRASH item=1484
leakscan CRASH item=1492
leakscan CRASH item=1505
leakscan CRASH item=1506
leakscan CRASH item=1526
leakscan CRASH item=1550
leakscan CRASH item=1611
leakscan CRASH item=1623
leakscan CRASH item=1627
leakscan CRASH item=1665
leakscan CRASH item=1739
leakscan CRASH item=1812
leakscan CRASH item=1813
leakscan CRASH item=1814
leakscan CRASH item=1815
leakscan CRASH item=1826
leakscan CRASH item=207
leakscan CRASH item=2773
leakscan CRASH item=2774
leakscan CRASH item=2785
leakscan CRASH item=38
leakscan CRASH item=994
leakscan LEAK item=1202
leakscan LEAK item=1203
leakscan LEAK item=1465
leakscan LEAK item=1889
